title: Narcissus blancoi
intro: Narcissus blancoi, also known as the white daffodil, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus blancoi include alkaloids, such as galantamine and lycorine, which have been found to possess neuroprotective and vasodilatory effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Narcissus blancoi is a species of flowering plant within the genus Narcissus and the family Amaryllidaceae. It belongs to the order Asparagales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Narcissus blancoi is further classified into the subfamily Amaryllidoideae and the tribe Narcisseae. The species is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine. Narcissus blancoi is also known as the 'white daffodil' due to its white flowers.
